title: Filtered latent-wear features with uncertainty instead of raw degradation ratios
summary: A recent F1 state-space paper models lap time as fuel plus latent tyre pace with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive robustness than a simple AR baseline.
rationale: A recent F1 state-space paper models lap time as fuel plus latent tyre pace with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive robustness than a simple AR baseline. Your current features approximate wear with ratios and short rolling windows; a filtered latent state, slope, and uncertainty should capture nonlinear fade and warm-up more causally.
implementation_hint: Per driver-race-stint, fit a simple causal state-space/Kalman filter on lap-time residuals after race-level normalization. Export filtered wear level, wear acceleration, reset shock after pit, and posterior variance as tabular features, with compound-specific process noise if feasible.
expected_effect: Better separation between genuine tyre fade and one-off slow laps from traffic or anomalies, improving borderline pit decisions.
risk: Misspecified filters can inject noise, and runtime may become nontrivial if applied naively to every stint.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
